sourceMovie.title: The Rocky Horror Picture Show
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Rocky Horror Picture Show works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Totally Killer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Rocky Horror Picture Show clicked for you, Totally Killer (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Rocky Horror Picture Show clicked for you, Beetlejuice (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Rocky Horror Picture Show?
faq.answer: Try Totally Killer, Beetlejuice, Being John Malkovich—each shares DNA with The Rocky Horror Picture Show while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Rocky Horror Picture Show?
faq.answer: The Rocky Horror Picture Show (1975) is best described as Comedy, Science Fiction, Fantasy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Rocky Horror Picture Show part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Rocky Horror Picture Show Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
